utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Hi Jane, good to see you again. How are you feeling since last week?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Honestly, kind of mixed. The mornings are still really heavy, but I noticed I had two days where I actually wanted to call my sister.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: That's a meaningful shift. Did you call her?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: I did, on Saturday. We talked for about an hour.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: How was your mood after that conversation, on a scale of zero to ten?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Maybe a six? It dipped again Sunday morning though.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Let's look at the thought records. You filled out five of seven this week, is that right?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Yeah. The two I missed were the days I just couldn't get out of bed.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Any cognitive distortions you noticed coming up repeatedly?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Catastrophizing, mostly about work. And mind reading, like assuming my manager is annoyed with me.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Good catch. Let's pick the work catastrophizing one and walk through it. What's the situation that triggered it?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: I missed a deadline by half a day and immediately thought I was going to be fired.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: And the evidence for and against that thought?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Evidence for: my manager replied with just 'ok'. Evidence against: I've never been written up, I had a positive review last quarter, and other people miss deadlines too.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: What's a more balanced thought?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Missing one deadline by half a day is unlikely to get me fired. My manager is probably just busy.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: How do you feel saying that?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: A little lighter. Maybe a seven instead of a two.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Any thoughts of self-harm or hopelessness this week?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: No active thoughts. Some passive 'what's the point' moments but nothing more than that.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Okay. For homework this week, let's keep the daily thought record and add one behavioral activation: schedule one social activity, even something small.

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: I can do coffee with my neighbor on Wednesday.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Perfect. We'll see each other next Tuesday at the same time.

patient.name: Jane Doe
diagnoses: F33.1 Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate

goals: Reduce PHQ-9 score from 16 to <10 within 12 weeks
Identify and reframe at least 3 cognitive distortions per week
treatment_plan.homework_last_session: Daily thought record for negative automatic thoughts

patient.previous_session_summary: Patient reported moderate depressive symptoms, completed 5 of 7 thought records. Discussed cognitive distortion of catastrophizing related to work.
